title: Differentiate quotients
description: Find the derivatives of quotients of basic functions.
topic_path: Differentiation: definition and basic derivative rules

hints:
In conclusion,

$f'(x)=-\dfrac{\cos(x)+x\sin(x)}{x^2}$

or any other equivalent form.

In conclusion,

$g'(x)=\dfrac{2x\cos(x)-\sin(x)}{2x\sqrt{x}}$

or any other equivalent form.

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{d}{dx}\left(\dfrac{\sin(x)}{\cos(x)}\right)=\sec^2(x)$

or any other equivalent form.

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[\dfrac{x^3}{\sin(x)}\right]=\dfrac{3x^2\sin(x)-x^3\cos(x)}{\sin^2(x)}$

or any other equivalent form.

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{dy}{dx}=-\dfrac{x\sin(x)+3\cos(x)}{x^4}$

or any other equivalent form.

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{dy}{dx}=\dfrac{\sin(x)-x\cos(x)}{\sin^2(x)}$

or any other equivalent form.

In conclusion,

$f'(x)=\dfrac{x(2-x)}{e^x}$

or any other equivalent form.

In conclusion,

$h'(x)=\dfrac{1-2\ln(x)}{x^3}$

or any other equivalent form.

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{d}{dx}\left(\dfrac{e^x}{\cos(x)}\right)=\dfrac{e^x(\cos(x)+\sin(x))}{\cos^2(x)}$

or any other equivalent form.

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{d}{dx}\left(\dfrac{\ln(x)}{\sqrt{x}}\right)=\dfrac{2-\ln(x)}{2x\sqrt{x}}$

or any other equivalent form.

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{dy}{dx}={\dfrac{1-2x}{2e^x\sqrt{x}}}$

or any other equivalent form.

In conclusion,

$f'(x)=\dfrac{\sin(x)-2x\cos(x)}{2\sqrt x\sin^2(x)}$

In conclusion,

$g'(x)=-\dfrac{1}{\sin^2(x)}$

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[\dfrac{x}{\cos(x)}\right]=\dfrac{\cos(x)+x\sin(x)}{\cos^2(x)}$

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{d}{dx}\left(\dfrac{\sin(x)}{x^2}\right)=\dfrac{x\cos(x)-2\sin(x)}{x^3}$

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{dy}{dx}=\dfrac{2x\cos(x)+x^2\sin(x)}{\cos^2(x)}$

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{dy}{dx}=\dfrac{\cos(x)+2x\sin(x)}{2\sqrt x\cos^2(x)}$

In conclusion,

$f'(x)=\dfrac{2x\ln(x)-x}{(\ln(x))^2}$

In conclusion,

$g'(x)=\dfrac{\cos(x)-\sin(x)}{e^x}$

In conclusion,

$h'(x)=\dfrac{-x\sin(x)\ln(x)-\cos(x)}{x(\ln(x))^2}$

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{d}{dx}\left(\dfrac{e^x}{\sqrt{x}}\right)=\dfrac{e^x\left(\sqrt{x}-\dfrac{1}{2\sqrt{x}}\right)}{x}$

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{d}{dx}\left(\dfrac{\ln(x)}{e^x}\right)=\dfrac{1-x\ln(x)}{xe^x}$

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{dy}{dx}=\dfrac{e^x(x-1)}{x^2}$

In conclusion,

$\dfrac{dy}{dx}=\dfrac{\sin(x)-x\ln(x)\cos(x)}{x\sin^2(x)}$
